summary.intent: Improve the visual and UX polish of the Planetfall game prototype: fix landmark pin contrast on the island, make navigation controls clearer and less overwhelming, and add looping background music with player controls.
summary.outcome: Landmark pin recolored to deep violet/cream/gold scheme; island HUD split into a short objective prompt and a separate controls legend; background music added with play/pause/mute/volume controls and autoplay-retry logic; both changes committed and pushed to origin/main.

repo: Project uses Three.js/Vite with a CSS custom-property palette: cyan for system UI, warm gold for artifacts/islands, dark translucent panels — cyan was a legacy agent choice, not the intended theme.

Entire checkpoint system attaches to git commits via a commit-message trailer (Entire-Checkpoint: <hash>), but only auto-attaches for Claude Code sessions; Codex sessions require manual `entire session attach` with the Codex session ID.
Static assets served from public/ are available at the root URL path in Vite (e.g. public/audio/foo.mp3 → /audio/foo.mp3).
PLAN.md is the authoritative project roadmap; README lags behind it. Next planned work is puzzle #1 (EVA Helmet / `recap` command).
island prompt logic in src/islandView.js was rebuilding control legend HTML on every animation frame tick — required a guard to prevent per-frame DOM churn.

code.finding: 3D hover outline ring color for island marker — changed from pale gold to match landmark pin theme
Island HUD prompt logic: split into bottom-center objective prompt and separate controls legend; Esc unlock handler added here alongside pointer-lock state management
Audio wiring: play/pause, mute, volume, loop, and first-interaction autoplay retry via capture-phase event listener
Looping audio element added here; custom music controls strip (icon, play/pause, mute, volume slider) also in markup

workflow: Chrome blocks audible autoplay before user interaction — the pattern for web games is: attempt autoplay on load, show a pulsing 'waiting' state on the play button, retry on first real click/key via a capture-phase listener.
Pointer-lock (first-person mouse-look) cannot be triggered by automated browser control — it requires a genuine user gesture, so visual verification of pointer-lock behavior must be done manually.
Entire session IDs for Codex sessions live in ~/.codex/session_index.jsonl, not in the repo's .entire/metadata; manual lookup needed to attach a checkpoint trailer after the fact.
When a commit is already pushed and needs an amended trailer, `entire session attach --force` amends HEAD, requiring a follow-up force push with lease protection.

friction: Automated browser control could not trigger pointer-lock (browser security policy), so the Esc-to-release flow could not be end-to-end tested in the session.
Screenshot/visual capture calls repeatedly timed out after page reloads, requiring fallback to DOM measurement queries to verify layout.
Chrome's autoplay policy blocks audible audio on page load regardless of the `autoplay` attribute — no workaround exists without starting muted.

Entire checkpoint trailer was missing from the second commit because the Codex session ID is not tracked the same way as Claude Code sessions; required digging through ~/.codex/session_index.jsonl to find the session ID for manual attach.
Entire CLI command names evolved during the project (e.g. `entire explain` vs `entire checkpoint explain`) causing several failed lookups before the correct form was found.
open_items: Broader HUD still contains old cyan accent variable in several places — intentionally left untouched during this marker/music pass.
Third-person camera mode discussed and deferred — would require a visible astronaut character, camera follow logic, and collision/grounding.
Puzzle mechanics not yet built; next concrete step per PLAN.md is puzzle #1 (EVA Helmet / `recap` command) as the template for remaining puzzles.
Command engine implementation still undecided (simulated vs real Entire commands).
Untracked .codex/ directory and HANDOFF.md left unstaged in every commit — their intended disposition is unclear.
`entire session attach` for the second commit (2847ddd) was in progress at the end of the transcript — unclear if it completed successfully.
